payload.title: Public distribution — Trust v0.1
payload.description:
Take Trust from local greenfield to publicly installable. Target: any developer or agent can find the repo, run one install command, point an editor at a Rust file, see the eval headline numbers, and have a strict-mode Trust file under cargo within 5 minutes.

Validation evidence (now in hand from RT-25 epic): vanilla 60% bug-ship rate → trust 0% across 3 vendors (Haiku, GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Flash). Time to make those numbers visible to people who aren't bri.

Phases:
- Prep: license, crate metadata, README for cold visitors (RT-55, RT-56)
- Channels: public GitHub + CI, crates.io publish, VS Code extension (RT-57, RT-58, RT-59)
- Story: extend WHY.md with eval numbers + neighbors comparison; draft launch comms (RT-60, RT-61)

Open questions for bri to resolve before relevant stories start:
- License: assuming dual MIT + Apache-2.0 (Rust ecosystem default). Confirm or override on RT-55.
- Editor scope: starting VS Code only (highest reach). Zed/JetBrains can come later if there's demand.
- cargo-trust-new project scaffolder: NOT in this epic — it's a feature, not distribution. File separately if wanted.
- Launch timing: RT-61 preps the comms but doesn't fire. Bri decides when to ship the HN/Reddit/Lobsters posts.
